title: Halasuru Someshwara Temple, Bangalore
tagline: Halasuru Someshwara Temple, Bangalore — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: Halasuru Someshwara Temple is a Hindu temple, located in the neighborhood of Halasuru in Bangalore, Karnataka, India.
overview.description: Halasuru Someshwara Temple is a Hindu temple, located in the neighborhood of Halasuru in Bangalore, Karnataka, India. It is one of the old temples in the city, dating back to the Chola period, and it is dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva. Major additions or modifications were made during the late Vijayanagara Empire period under the rule of Hiriya Kempe Gowda II. Someshwara temples were built by the Cholas in Karnataka. During that period, we see Tamil inscriptions as a few Mudaliars (Tuluva Vellala) were settled.

topPlaces.name: Halasuru metro station
topPlaces.description: Halasuru is an elevated metro station on the east–west corridor of the Purple Line of Namma Metro in Bengaluru, India. It was opened to the public on 20 October 2011.

topPlaces.name: Holy Trinity Church, Bengaluru
topPlaces.description: Holy Trinity Church, located at Trinity circle at the east end of the MG Road, is an Anglican church and a major landmark in Bengaluru. It was built in 1851, for the British Regiment stationed there.

topPlaces.name: Murphy Town, Bengaluru
topPlaces.description: Murphy Town, officially Hoysala Nagara and historically referred to as Knoxpete is a suburb located near Bangalore Cantonment, India. It is one of the oldest planned suburbs of the Cantonment, and was earlier known as Knoxpete, and was later changed to Hoysala Nagar.

topPlaces.name: Ramakrishna Math, Ulsoor
topPlaces.description: Ramakrishna Math, Ulsoor is a branch of Ramakrishna Math and Ramakrishna Mission located at Ulsoor, Bangalore, India. Other than Ulsoor, there is another Ramakrishna Mission branch in Bangalore located at Bull Temple Road.

topPlaces.name: Trinity metro station
topPlaces.description: Trinity is an elevated metro station on the East-West corridor of the Purple Line of Namma Metro in Bangalore, India. It was opened to the public on 20 October 2011.

topPlaces.name: Ulsoor Lake
topPlaces.description: Ulsoor Lake or Halasuru Lake, one of the biggest lakes in Bangalore, is located on the eastern side of the city. It derives its name from the name of the locality it is situated, namely, Halasuru, close to M G Road.

topPlaces.name: Ulsoor
topPlaces.description: Ulsoor, officially known as Halasuru, is one of the oldest neighbourhoods in the city of Bengaluru. It is in central Bengaluru, and begins near the eastern terminus of Mahatma Gandhi Raste.

topPlaces.name: East Parade Church
topPlaces.description: The East Parade Church, consecrated in 1865, is located on Mahatma Gandhi Road, in the Bangalore Cantonment. The church comes under the Karnataka Central Diocese of the Church of South India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Halasuru Someshwara Temple, Bangalore?
faq.a: The best time to visit Halasuru Someshwara Temple, Bangalore is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Halasuru Someshwara Temple, Bangalore?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (26 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (6 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Halasuru Someshwara Temple, Bangalore?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Halasuru Someshwara Temple, Bangalore at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Halasuru Someshwara Temple, Bangalore?
faq.a: Stays in Halasuru Someshwara Temple, Bangalore start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Halasuru Someshwara Temple, Bangalore?
faq.a: The top attractions are Halasuru metro station, Holy Trinity Church, Bengaluru, Murphy Town, Bengaluru, Ramakrishna Math, Ulsoor, Trinity metro station.
